brief_summary: Four-panel taxonomy strip showing each sensing lineage with a small mechanism glyph. MX: two leaves meeting with a contact dot ('contact closure / binary'). Topre: dome over a capacitive pad with sense waves ('electrocapacitive / analog to binary'). Hall-effect: coral-emphasized magnet over a sensor pad with bending field lines ('magnetic field / analog exposed'). Optical: LED → light beam → photodetector with a blade interrupting the beam ('light-gate / mostly binary').
data_sources: magnetic-switches-deep-dive.mdx § 'Where Hall-effect sits in the four-lineage taxonomy' — 'MX contact-closure is the default ... Topre electrocapacitive ... reads spring compression as a change in capacitance over a printed pad ... Hall-effect magnetic ... a magnet on the stem, a Hall sensor on the PCB, a continuous voltage available to the firmware ... Optical light-gate ... a beam of light from an LED ... interrupted by a blade or slot on the stem.'
